title: Myriactis minuscula
intro: Myriactis minuscula, also known as dwarf false willow or hairy bugloss, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Myriactis minuscula include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Herbal preparations made from Myriactis minuscula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
